_meta.description: Curated directory of Cincinnati civic organizations, surfaced contextually alongside relevant data. Maintained by hand — no API will replace local knowledge of who works on what in this city.

service_types.direct_service: Direct Service — provides help directly to individuals
service_types.organizing: Organizing & Advocacy — builds community power for systemic change

_meta.note: Verified April 2026. Some contact details may change — always confirm on the org's website before referring residents.

organizations.name: Legal Aid Society of Greater Cincinnati
organizations.mission: Free civil legal aid to low-income residents — eviction defense, foreclosure prevention, landlord-tenant disputes. One of the most critical resources for residents facing housing instability.

organizations.name: Housing Opportunities Made Equal (HOME Cincinnati)
organizations.mission: Fair housing enforcement, counseling, and education for renters and homebuyers facing discrimination. Files fair housing complaints on behalf of residents.

organizations.name: Over-the-Rhine Community Housing
organizations.mission: Preserves and develops permanently affordable housing in OTR and adjacent neighborhoods. Operates a community land trust to keep housing off the speculative market.

organizations.name: Price Hill Will
organizations.mission: Neighborhood CDC driving community-led revitalization in Price Hill — housing rehab, small business support, arts, and community organizing.

organizations.name: Communities Act Now (CAN)
organizations.mission: Organizing against discriminatory lending, predatory real estate, biased policing, and reckless displacement in communities of color. Works through neighborhood-level member networks.

organizations.name: Cincinnati-Hamilton County Community Action Agency
organizations.mission: Emergency rent and utility assistance for households in crisis, plus long-term stability programs for low-income families.

organizations.name: Greater Cincinnati Water Works — Lead Program
organizations.mission: Manages the 100% city-funded lead service line replacement program and provides free water testing. If your service line is lead, GCWW replaces it at no cost.

organizations.name: Cincinnati Health Department — Lead Poisoning Prevention
organizations.mission: Free blood lead testing for children, free water filters, and coordination of lead abatement for homes with children under 6.

organizations.name: Groundwork Ohio River Valley
organizations.mission: Environmental justice work rooted in youth workforce development, community air quality monitoring, and building green infrastructure in under-resourced neighborhoods.

organizations.name: Green Umbrella
organizations.mission: Greater Cincinnati's regional sustainability network — convenes organizations across sectors on climate, environmental justice, and green infrastructure initiatives.

organizations.name: Cincinnati NAACP
organizations.mission: Racial equity, civil rights, and police accountability through advocacy, organizing, and legal action. Runs a mobile civic engagement unit for voter registration and rights education.

organizations.name: Freestore Foodbank
organizations.mission: The region's largest hunger-relief organization — food pantries, mobile markets, and school-based programs serving 110,000+ people annually.

organizations.name: Society of St. Vincent de Paul Cincinnati
organizations.mission: Parish-based network providing food, emergency financial assistance, and utility support. 13 locations across Cincinnati.

organizations.name: Sustainable Cincy
organizations.mission: Grassroots coalition for equitable transit access, walkable neighborhoods, and sustainable development across Greater Cincinnati.

organizations.name: ChangingGears
organizations.mission: Makes vehicle ownership accessible to people moving out of poverty — removing the transportation barrier that keeps people from jobs and opportunity.

organizations.name: LISC Cincinnati
organizations.mission: Local Initiatives Support Corporation — capital and technical assistance to community developers and CDCs building affordable housing and neighborhood infrastructure.

organizations.name: Cincinnati Development Fund
organizations.mission: Community development financial institution providing capital and technical assistance to small businesses and community developers in underserved markets.

organizations.name: Greater Cincinnati Voter Collaborative
organizations.mission: Nonpartisan alliance running year-round voter registration, civic training, and neighborhood engagement to close voter participation gaps.

organizations.name: League of Women Voters — Cincinnati Area
organizations.mission: Nonpartisan civic organization encouraging informed participation — voter guides, candidate forums, and policy advocacy on democracy and good governance issues.
